title: Top-Only Dynamic Depth Routing Revisit
research_question: If dynamic depth routing is useful at all on the current frontier, does it only belong at the very top of the stack in a much lighter form?

candidates.name: v1-top1-two-sources-token
candidates.goal: Test the lightest plausible dynamic depth selector.
candidates.hypothesis: Only the very last layer may benefit from choosing between the current stream and one earlier source.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether top-only routing survives when confined to the smallest viable scope.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the cleanest way to salvage the faint Q3 signal without repeating the broad late-layer failure.
candidates.description: apply AttnRes-lite only to the last layer with two source states

candidates.name: v2-top2-two-sources-token
candidates.goal: Test whether the clean Q3 signal wants two top layers but still fewer source choices.
candidates.hypothesis: The earlier Q3 near-miss may have been harmed by giving the router too many source options.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether fewer choices make top-only routing safer.
candidates.why_worthy: This directly turns the Q3 result into a sharper second-generation test.
candidates.description: apply AttnRes-lite to the top two layers with two source states

candidates.name: v3-top2-shared-routing
candidates.goal: Test whether a cheaper top-only router is enough.
candidates.hypothesis: Top-only routing may work with shared sequence-level routing even though shared routing failed when applied broadly.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether top-only routing wants coarse control rather than token-wise flexibility.
candidates.why_worthy: This isolates the difference between broad shared routing failure and a narrower top-only use case.
candidates.description: apply shared-scalar AttnRes-lite only to the top two layers

candidates.name: v4-top1-three-sources-token
candidates.goal: Let the last layer use a slightly richer depth choice without broadening placement.
candidates.hypothesis: The final layer may be the one place where a richer multi-source selector is useful.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether the main issue was placement rather than source richness.
candidates.why_worthy: This cleanly separates “too many layers” from “too many source states.”
candidates.description: apply three-source token-wise AttnRes-lite only to the final layer

candidates.name: v5-top2-routing-plus-cheap-skips
candidates.goal: Test the only routing combo that still looks scientifically justified after tranche Q.
candidates.hypothesis: Top-only dynamic routing may stack with shared skip gates even though broad AttnRes-lite did not stack with the full routing package.
candidates.what_it_teaches: Whether there is still a narrow path for routing-family interaction to matter.
candidates.why_worthy: This is the only justified combo revisit because it uses the one AttnRes-lite placement that did not collapse.
candidates.description: apply top-two-layer AttnRes-lite together with shared scalar skip gates
